symptoms.name: Stringing / Oozing
symptoms.desc: Fine hairs or threads between parts

causes.title: Retraction distance too short
causes.detail: Filament oozes through the nozzle during travel moves. Retraction pulls it back — increase until stringing stops.
causes.fix: Direct drive: try 0.8–1.2mm. Increase in 0.2mm steps. Too much causes jams in flexibles.

causes.title: Nozzle temperature too high
causes.detail: Hotter filament is more fluid and drips more during travel. Drop temp until stringing reduces without losing layer adhesion.
causes.fix: Reduce by 5°C increments. Stay above minimum rated temp for the material.

causes.title: Travel speed too slow
causes.detail: Slow travel gives the nozzle more time to ooze between positions. Faster = less drip.
causes.fix: Set travel speed to 200–250mm/s. Bambu printers handle this comfortably.

causes.title: Cooling fan too low (PETG)
causes.detail: PETG needs moderate cooling — too little allows extruded strings to stay soft and stretch further.
causes.fix: PETG: set fan to 40–60%. Too high causes poor layer bonding; too low causes stringing.

causes.title: Wet TPU filament (TPU only)
causes.detail: TPU absorbs moisture rapidly. Wet TPU produces constant oozing that looks like extreme stringing — retraction and temperature adjustments alone cannot fix moisture-caused stringing.
causes.fix: Dry TPU at 60–65°C for 6–8h before printing. Check for hissing or crackling sounds at the nozzle — these confirm moisture. 85A should be dried every session.

symptoms.name: Warping / Bed Adhesion
symptoms.desc: Corners or edges lifting off the bed

causes.title: Build plate not cleaned with IPA
causes.detail: Fingerprints and skin oils are the #1 cause of adhesion failures. A clean plate is non-negotiable.
causes.fix: Wipe with 90%+ isopropyl alcohol and a clean cloth before every print. Avoid touching the surface.

causes.title: No brim on high-shrinkage material
causes.detail: ABS, ASA, PA and PC shrink as they cool. Without a brim anchoring the corners, they lift.
causes.fix: Add 6–10mm brim. PA/PC: use 10–12mm. Enable in Bambu Studio → Process → Others → Brim.

causes.title: No enclosure — temperature instability
causes.detail: Drafts and cold ambient air cause rapid uneven cooling. Shrinkage stress builds up and lifts corners.
causes.fix: Use an enclosed printer or build an enclosure. Eliminate air flow around the printer.

causes.title: Bed temperature too low
causes.detail: The bed needs to keep the first layers slightly warm and adhered throughout the print.
causes.fix: Increase bed temp by 5°C increments up to material maximum. See material profile for range.

causes.title: First layer speed too fast
causes.detail: Printing the first layer fast doesn't give the filament time to bond to the build surface.
causes.fix: Set initial layer speed to 25–35mm/s. Bambu default is 20mm/s — keep it there.

symptoms.name: Layer Separation
symptoms.desc: Layers splitting apart or not bonding

causes.title: Nozzle temperature too low
causes.detail: Layers must be hot enough to fuse together. Too cold = brittle inter-layer bonds that snap under stress.
causes.fix: Increase by 5°C increments. Don't exceed material max rated temp.

causes.title: Print speed too high
causes.detail: High speed reduces dwell time between layers — not enough time to fuse properly.
causes.fix: Reduce outer wall speed by 20%. Try 40–50mm/s as a baseline for problem materials.

causes.title: Cooling fan too aggressive
causes.detail: Rapid cooling solidifies each layer before the next can bond to it, especially for high-temp materials.
causes.fix: ABS/ASA: set to 0–10%. PETG: max 60%. PA/PC: 0–20%. Reduce in 10% steps.

causes.title: Wet filament (moisture-sensitive materials)
causes.detail: Moisture turns to steam at print temps, creating micro-bubbles that weaken every layer bond.
causes.fix: Dry filament per the material spec before printing. PA: 70°C/8h. PC: 80°C/5h.

causes.title: Layer height too tall for nozzle
causes.detail: Layers exceeding ~75% of nozzle diameter lack enough pressure to bond to the layer below.
causes.fix: Keep layer height at 25–75% of nozzle diameter. 0.4mm nozzle → max 0.30mm layers.

symptoms.name: Under-extrusion / Gaps
symptoms.desc: Gaps in walls, weak infill, or missing material

causes.title: Max volumetric speed too high
causes.detail: The hotend can only melt filament so fast. Exceeding the material's MVS causes grinding and gaps.
causes.fix: Check this configurator's filament panel for the correct MVS for your material + nozzle. Set it in Bambu Studio filament settings.

causes.title: Nozzle partially clogged
causes.detail: Buildup inside the nozzle restricts flow. Common after abrasive filaments or heat-creep events.
causes.fix: Do a cold pull: heat to 230°C, push filament through, cool to 90°C, pull out fast. Repeat 2–3×.

causes.title: Flow ratio too low
causes.detail: The flow multiplier scales how much filament is pushed. Below 0.95 and walls will be visibly thin.
causes.fix: Run flow calibration in Bambu Studio. Typical range: 0.95–1.05. Adjust in 0.02 steps.

causes.title: Temperature too low for current speed
causes.detail: Printing fast requires more heat to melt filament in time. Speed and temp must scale together.
causes.fix: Increase nozzle temp by 5°C or reduce print speed by 20%.

causes.title: Wet filament
causes.detail: Steam from moisture creates bubbles in the filament stream, interrupting consistent extrusion.
causes.fix: Dry filament at the recommended temperature. Even a few hours in air degrades PA/PC significantly.

symptoms.name: Over-extrusion / Blobs
symptoms.desc: Too much material, blobs, or thick rough walls

causes.title: Flow ratio too high
causes.detail: Too much filament pushed out — walls are over-wide and blobs form at direction changes.
causes.fix: Run flow calibration. Reduce in 0.02 steps (e.g. 1.00 → 0.98 → 0.96) until walls are clean.

causes.title: Nozzle temperature too high
causes.detail: Excess heat makes filament too runny — it spreads more than the slicer intends.
causes.fix: Reduce by 5°C increments. Stay above the material minimum or layer adhesion suffers.

causes.title: Pressure advance not calibrated
causes.detail: Without pressure advance, filament continues to ooze when the nozzle slows at corners — creating blobs.
causes.fix: Run Bambu Studio pressure advance calibration. Typical PLA: 0.02–0.05. PETG: 0.04–0.08.

symptoms.name: Elephant Foot
symptoms.desc: First layer flares out wider than the rest

causes.title: Z offset too low (nozzle too close)
causes.detail: Nozzle squishing too hard spreads the first layer outward beyond the intended footprint.
causes.fix: Increase Z offset by 0.02–0.05mm increments until first layer is correctly sized.

causes.title: Elephant foot compensation not set
causes.detail: Bambu Studio has a built-in compensation parameter designed specifically for this issue.
causes.fix: Set Elephant Foot Compensation to 0.10–0.20mm in Bambu Studio → Process → Quality.

causes.title: Bed temperature too high
causes.detail: An overly hot bed keeps the first layer too soft and pliable — it spreads under the nozzle's pressure.
causes.fix: Reduce bed temp by 5°C. First layer adhesion is usually fine down to the material minimum.

symptoms.name: Ringing / Ghosting
symptoms.desc: Ripple or wave pattern near sharp features

causes.title: Outer wall speed too high
causes.detail: Vibrations from rapid direction changes show up as ripple patterns on vertical surfaces.
causes.fix: Reduce outer wall speed to 50–80mm/s. A1/A1 Mini: keep below 100mm/s.

causes.title: Acceleration too high
causes.detail: Aggressive acceleration causes the motion system to ring out at each direction change.
causes.fix: Reduce outer wall acceleration. A1/A1 Mini: try 3000–5000mm/s². CoreXY (X1C/P1S): 6000–10000.

causes.title: Loose belts
causes.detail: Slack in the motion belts amplifies vibration artifacts significantly.
causes.fix: Check belt tension. Bambu printers: run the belt tension check in the touchscreen maintenance menu.

symptoms.name: Poor First Layer
symptoms.desc: First layer not sticking, uneven, or has gaps

causes.title: Build plate not cleaned
causes.detail: Fingerprints, grease, or dust prevent proper adhesion. This is the cause in ~60% of first-layer failures.
causes.fix: Wipe with 90%+ isopropyl alcohol and a lint-free cloth. Don't touch the plate afterward.

causes.title: Z offset too high — nozzle too far
causes.detail: If the nozzle is too far from the bed, filament doesn't squish in and bond to the surface.
causes.fix: Run Auto Bed Leveling from the touchscreen. Fine-tune Z offset down by 0.02mm steps.

causes.title: Glue not applied (required materials)
causes.detail: ABS, ASA, PA, PC need glue for reliable adhesion — especially on engineering plate.
causes.fix: Apply a thin even glue stick layer. Re-apply every 3–5 prints or when adhesion degrades.

causes.title: First layer speed too fast
causes.detail: Fast first layers don't give filament time to properly bond with the build surface.
causes.fix: Keep initial layer speed at 20–35mm/s maximum.

causes.title: Bed temperature too low
causes.detail: Material-specific minimum bed temps are required for adhesion. Cold bed = poor bond.
causes.fix: Check material's recommended bed temp range in the filament settings panel. Let the bed fully reach temp before printing.

symptoms.name: Flexible Filament Jam (TPU)
symptoms.desc: Soft TPU buckling, grinding, or jamming in the extruder

causes.title: Print speed too high
causes.detail: Soft TPU cannot transmit push force at high speeds — it compresses instead. This is the most common cause of jams in 85A/90A.
causes.fix: TPU 85A: reduce to 15–20 mm/s. TPU 90A: reduce to 25–30 mm/s. Reduce MVS cap: 2 mm³/s for 85A, 3–5 mm³/s for 90A.

causes.title: Retraction too long or too fast
causes.detail: Flexible filament stretches under retraction tension. Excess retraction builds up stress in the melt zone and causes a plug to form.
causes.fix: TPU 85A: set retraction to 0mm. TPU 90A: set to 0–0.5mm max. Retraction speed: 20–25 mm/s.

causes.title: Extruder idler tension too high
causes.detail: Default extruder tension is set for rigid filaments. It flattens soft TPU — the widened filament then jams in the PTFE tube or cold zone.
causes.fix: Loosen the extruder idler tension screw. On Bambu printers, this screw is behind the toolhead. Reduce tension until the gears grip without visibly deforming the filament.

causes.title: Filament routed through PTFE tube (85A)
causes.detail: TPU 85A cannot be pushed reliably through long PTFE sections — it buckles inside the tube before reaching the hotend.
causes.fix: On Bambu X/P series: load TPU 85A from directly above the toolhead — do not use the AMS or route through the rear PTFE tube.

causes.title: Nozzle too small (85A only)
causes.detail: TPU 85A requires a 0.6mm or larger nozzle. Smaller nozzles create too much back-pressure for the soft filament to overcome.
causes.fix: Switch to a 0.6mm or 0.8mm nozzle for TPU 85A. 0.4mm and below are not compatible.
